name: General Formalised Framework for Reasoning About Display Calculi

upstream_environment.notes: The source paper states that the framework is encoded in Coq/Rocq 8.18.0.

locator.notes: No public repository or archive URL is registered yet in this project; the artifact is therefore tracked as source-described until an upstream locator and revision can be pinned.

claims.description: The paper describes a constructive Coq/Rocq formalization showing that Belnap conditions C2-C8 imply cut-elimination for the framework.
The paper describes instantiated display calculi for classical propositional logic, tense logic Kt, and non-commutative non-associative Lambek calculus.
The paper describes a formal proof of decidability for CPL inside the display-calculus framework.
The paper describes constructive proofs enabling extraction of cut-elimination programs.
The paper reports a Coq codebase comprising more than 15000 lines.

integration_targets.description: Locate the public upstream proof environment, then pin URL, revision, and artifact hashes.
Build the upstream proof environment with the declared Coq/Rocq 8.18.0 toolchain.
Reproduce one CPL cut-elimination or decidability example as a Theorem DNA integration test.

checks.description: The upstream artifact registration validates against schema/upstream_artifact.schema.json.
The source DOI is present in data/papers/foundational-formalization-candidates.json.
Pending until a public locator and revision are pinned.
Pending until the upstream proof environment can be checked out.

verification.notes: This registration intentionally avoids duplicating the full upstream constructive framework.
